title: Rubus acalyphaceus
taxonomy: Rubus acalyphaceus is classified within the plant kingdom, specifically within the family Rosaceae. This genus, Rubus, is a group of flowering plants that includes brambles and raspberries. The species is further categorized as a member of the order Rosales, which contains various flowering plant families. Rubus acalyphaceus is a species of bramble, and its classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic relationships to other species within the Rubus genus. The classification of Rubus acalyphaceus is continually being refined through ongoing research and advances in botanical science.

side_effects: Rubus acalyphaceus is a plant species that may cause irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. The sap of the plant can cause skin irritation, and prolonged exposure may lead to more severe reactions. Ingesting any part of the plant can cause gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and vomiting. Contact with the plant's sap may also cause eye irritation and redness. It's essential to handle Rubus acalyphaceus with caution and avoid exposure to its sap and leaves.
intro: Rubus acalyphaceus, also known as Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the Himalayan region. The health benefits of Rubus acalyphaceus include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against various diseases and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Rubus acalyphaceus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rubus acalyphaceus are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
